title: artemisia borealis subsp. richardsoniana
intro: Artemisia borealis subsp. richardsoniana, also known as Richardson's wormwood or prairie sagewort, is a subspecies of perennial herb in the daisy family native to the prairies of the northwestern United States and southwestern Canada. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antihelmintic properties. The health benefits of Artemisia borealis subsp. richardsoniana include relief from digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to act as a natural insect repellent. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for medicinal use, and its essential oil is also used in aromatherapy and skincare products.
